title: Upon The Same. (To The Detractor.)
content:
I ask'd thee oft what poets thou hast read,
    And lik'st the best. Still thou reply'st: The dead.
    I shall, ere long, with green turfs cover'd be;
    Then sure thou'lt like or thou wilt envy me.
meaning:
"Upon The Same. (To The Detractor.)" is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
